skill.name: remotion-render
skill.description: Render videos from React/Remotion component code via inference.sh. Pass TSX code, get MP4. Supports all Remotion APIs: useCurrentFrame, useVideoConfig, spring, interpolate, AbsoluteFill, Sequence. Configurable resolution, FPS, duration, codec. Use for: programmatic video generation, animated graphics, motion design, data-driven videos, React animations to video. Triggers: remotion, render video from code, tsx to video, react video, programmatic video, remotion render, code to video, animated video, motion graphics code, react animation video
skill.summary: Render Remotion TSX components to MP4 video instantly using the inference.sh cloud service, with support for animations, sequences, and custom resolutions.
skill.author: inference-sh-skills

security_audit.summary: All 41 static analysis findings are false positives. The external_commands detections (32 findings) flagged markdown code fence backticks as Ruby shell execution, but SKILL.md is a documentation-only file containing bash examples in markdown code blocks. The network detections (6 findings) flagged legitimate documentation URLs for inference.sh and remotion.dev, which are the core service endpoints this skill integrates with. The high-severity blocker findings (3 detections of 'weak cryptographic algorithm') have no basis in the file content, which contains no cryptographic code. This is a legitimate documentation skill for rendering Remotion videos via a cloud service. The permissive Bash(belt *) tool permission is by design for CLI-based skills.

high_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Detected
high_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' at SKILL.md lines 3 and 35. The file contains no cryptographic code. Line 3 is the YAML frontmatter description listing Remotion API functions. Line 35 is a table header in the input schema section. This is a pattern-matching false positive with no actual security relevance.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: Ruby Shell Backtick Execution
medium_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 32 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' in SKILL.md. All detections correspond to markdown code fence backticks (```) used to delimit bash command examples and code blocks. SKILL.md is a documentation-only markdown file. The analyzer confused markdown fence syntax with Ruby's backtick command execution operator. No actual shell command execution exists in the skill.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Hardcoded URL Detected
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 6 hardcoded URLs as LOW risk network indicators. All URLs serve legitimate documentation and service purposes: inference.sh (the rendering platform API), remotion.dev (official Remotion documentation), and a cloud.inference.sh image URL (skill screenshot). These URLs are necessary references for the skill's core functionality and do not represent data exfiltration or malicious network activity.

content.user_title: Render Videos from React and Remotion Code
content.value_statement: Creating programmatic videos from React code requires complex infrastructure and setup. This skill lets you render Remotion TSX components to MP4 video instantly using the inference.sh cloud service, with full support for animations, sequences, and custom configurations.
actual_capabilities: Render Remotion TSX components to MP4 video using the inference.sh cloud service
Support standard Remotion APIs including useCurrentFrame, useVideoConfig, spring, interpolate, AbsoluteFill, and Sequence
Configure video resolution, frame rate, duration, and output codec
Pass custom props to React components for data-driven and dynamic video content
Use Sequence components for multi-scene animations with staggered timing
Stream render progress in real time for long video generation jobs
limitations: Requires internet access and an inference.sh account with the belt CLI installed
Inline TSX code must be self-contained without external npm dependencies or local files
Video rendering speed depends on inference.sh cloud service capacity and current load
Does not support local Remotion rendering with custom bundler configuration or plugins

use_cases.title: Automated Social Media Content
use_cases.description: Marketing teams generate branded animated videos for social media platforms by writing Remotion components programmatically, removing the need for manual video editing tools.

use_cases.title: Data-Driven Video Reports
use_cases.description: Analysts render animated data visualizations as video files by passing dynamic datasets as props to Remotion chart components.

use_cases.title: Rapid Motion Prototyping
use_cases.description: Designers preview and refine motion concepts quickly without setting up a full local Remotion development environment with bundlers and renderers.

prompt_templates.title: Create a basic fade-in text video
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 3-second 1080p video that shows 'Hello World' fading in from black. Use a black background with white text centered on screen.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user wanting to create a simple text animation

prompt_templates.title: Create an animated progress counter
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 5-second 1080p video showing a number counting from 0 to 100 with a status label below it. Use a dark background with bright colored text.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wanting to create a loading animation or progress indicator

prompt_templates.title: Create a data-driven bar chart video
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 10-second 1080p video that animates a bar chart. Pass sample sales data as props. Each bar should grow with a spring animation. Include axis labels and a title at the top.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wanting to generate animated charts from data

prompt_templates.title: Create a multi-scene product demo
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 15-second 1080p product demo video with three scenes using Sequence. Scene one shows the product name fading in. Scene two displays key features with slide-in animations. Scene three shows a call to action with a pulse animation. Pass product data as props.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user creating a structured multi-scene promotional video

output_examples.input: Render a 3-second 1080p video with white 'Hello World' text fading in on a black background
output_examples.output: An MP4 video file showing white text centered on a black background. The text smoothly fades from transparent to fully visible over the first second, then remains visible for the remaining two seconds.

output_examples.input: Render a 5-second 1080p video with a percentage counter animating from 0 to 100 percent
output_examples.output: An MP4 video file showing a large percentage number counting from 0 to 100 in smooth 60 frames per second animation. A smaller 'Loading...' label is displayed below the counter. Both elements are on a dark background.

output_examples.input: Render a 4-second video with three sequential scenes using Sequence components
output_examples.output: An MP4 video file with three distinct scenes playing one after another. Each scene fades in individually. The words 'First', 'Second', and 'Third' appear in sequence with staggered timing on a black background.

best_practices: Keep TSX code concise and self-contained to avoid syntax errors when embedding inline in JSON strings
Test animations with short durations of 2 to 3 seconds first before rendering longer videos
Use escape sequences properly for quotes and backslashes when embedding TSX code inside JSON input
anti_patterns: Including external imports beyond remotion and react will cause render failures on the cloud service
Using very long video durations without testing shorter versions first wastes API credits and time
Embedding sensitive data such as API keys or passwords in component props sent to the rendering service

faq.question: What is inference.sh?
faq.answer: inference.sh is a cloud service that runs applications on demand. This skill uses it to render Remotion videos without requiring local infrastructure.

faq.question: Do I need to install anything to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need the belt CLI tool. Install it by running: npx skills add belt-sh/cli

faq.question: What video formats and codecs are supported?
faq.answer: The service supports multiple codecs including h264 for MP4 output. You can configure the codec parameter in the input schema.

faq.question: How do I pass data to my video component?
faq.answer: Use the props field in the input JSON object. The props object is passed directly to your React component as its props.

faq.question: Can I use external fonts or images in my video?
faq.answer: Support is limited. Use inline styles and data URIs for assets. External resources may not be accessible from the rendering environment.

faq.question: What happens if my code has an error?
faq.answer: The service returns an error message describing the problem. Common errors include syntax issues, missing imports, and invalid component structure.
